symbols.name: CursorSdkErrorOptions
SdkErrorContext
AuthenticationError
symbols.jsDoc:
Authentication-related errors (401).
Examples: Invalid API key, not logged in, insufficient permissions.

symbols.name: ConfigurationError
symbols.jsDoc:
Configuration errors (400, 404).
Examples: Bad API key, invalid model name, invalid request parameters.

symbols.name: CursorAgentError
symbols.jsDoc:
Backward-compatible Agent SDK error root.
New code should usually check `err instanceof CursorSdkError`, but existing
`CursorAgentError` checks continue to work for all current SDK errors.

symbols.name: CursorSdkError
symbols.jsDoc:
Base error class for all Cursor SDK errors.
This can wrap internal ConnectError objects from gRPC calls, or represent
SDK-level errors (like API key exchange failures) that occur before any RPC.

symbols.name: convertConnectError
symbols.signature: export declare function convertConnectError(error: ConnectError): CursorAgentError;
symbols.jsDoc:
Convert a ConnectError to a user-friendly SDK error.
Extracts the ErrorDetails proto, reads the user-facing message,
and wraps it in an appropriate SDK error class.

symbols.name: convertError
symbols.signature: export declare function convertError(err: unknown): CursorAgentError | Error;
symbols.jsDoc:
Convert a generic Error to an SDK error if it represents a ConnectError.
Handles cases where errors may be wrapped or serialized.

symbols.name: IntegrationNotConnectedError
symbols.jsDoc:
The caller has not connected the SCM integration for the target
repository's provider. Distinct from `[repository_access]`, which means
the integration is connected but doesn't cover that repo.

symbols.name: NetworkError
symbols.jsDoc:
Network/server errors (503, 504, etc.).
Examples: Service unavailable, timeout, internal server errors.

symbols.name: RateLimitError
symbols.jsDoc:
Rate limiting errors (429).
Examples: Too many requests, usage limits exceeded.

symbols.name: UnknownAgentError
symbols.jsDoc: Unknown or unclassified errors.

symbols.name: wrapSdkError
symbols.signature: export declare function wrapSdkError(err: unknown, context?: SdkErrorContext): CursorSdkError;

symbols.name: AgentDefinition
AgentMessage
AgentOperationOptions
AgentOptions
CursorRequestOptions
symbols.jsDoc:
Options for cloud-only account/catalog operations (Cursor.me, Cursor.models.list,
Cursor.repositories.list). If `apiKey` is omitted, falls back to
`process.env.CURSOR_API_KEY`.

symbols.name: GetAgentMessagesOptions
GetAgentOptions
symbols.jsDoc:
Options for `Agent.get` / `Agent.archive` / `Agent.unarchive` /
`Agent.delete`.
Runtime is auto-detected from the agent ID: IDs that start with `"bc-"`
route to the Cursor cloud API, everything else routes to the local store.
- `cwd` is used when routing to the local store (defaults to `process.cwd()`).
- `apiKey` is used when routing to the cloud API (falls back to
`process.env.CURSOR_API_KEY`).

canonical `{ id, params }` that can be passed straight to `createAgent`.

symbols.jsDoc:
Account and platform-level operations. Namespaced separately from `Agent`
because these concerns (the calling user, available models, connected
repositories) aren't scoped to a specific agent.

symbols.name: SDKAgent.model
symbols.jsDoc:
The agent's current model selection. Updated after each successful
`send({ model })`; `undefined` until something sets it.

symbols.name: SDKAgent.send
symbols.signature: send(message: string | SDKUserMessage, options?: SendOptions): Promise<Run>;

symbols.name: SDKAgentInfo.archived
symbols.jsDoc:
True when the agent has been archived via `Agent.archive(...)`. Distinct
from `status` (which reflects the most recent run's execution state).
Backwards-compatible optional field; undefined on older shapes.

symbols.name: SendOptions.local.force
symbols.jsDoc:
Expire the currently active persisted run, if any, before starting
this message as a new follow-up run. Recovery path for local agents
left wedged after a crashed CLI process. Cloud enforces a busy-run
check server-side (`409 agent_busy`), so no equivalent is needed.

symbols.name: SendOptions.local
symbols.jsDoc:
Per-send options that only apply to local agents. Nested to make the
local-only scope explicit at the type level — cloud callers cannot
pass these fields.

symbols.name: SDKRequestMessage
AgentOptions.cloud.env
AgentOptions.cloud.repos
AgentOptions.cloud
AgentOptions.local.settingSources
symbols.jsDoc:
Ambient Cursor settings layers to load from the local filesystem.
Local agents only. On cloud, `project` / `team` / `plugins` are
always on and `user` / `mdm` / `local` have no VM equivalent, so
this field is gated to the local shape.

symbols.name: AgentOptions.local
AgentOptions.model
symbols.jsDoc:
Model selection (`{ id, params? }`). Required for local agents; optional
for cloud (the server resolves the caller's configured default when
omitted). Use `Cursor.models.list()` to discover valid selections.

symbols.name: AgentOptions.name
symbols.jsDoc:
Optional human-readable name for the agent, surfaced as the `title` in
`Agent.list()` / `Agent.get()`. Cloud agents auto-generate a name from
the first prompt when this is omitted; local agents fall back to a
generic default.
